department.name: Department of Economics
department.college: College of Family, Home, and Social Sciences

academicRequirements.gradeRequirements: Economics majors must earn C- or higher in MATH 112 and ECON 110, 378, 380, 381, 382, & 388 with passing grades in other economics courses
academicRequirements.minorRequirements: Minors need C- or higher in MATH 112, ECON 110, 380, & 381
academicRequirements.recommendedCourseLoad: No more than 3 economics courses per semester; 2 courses recommended
academicRequirements.challengeExams: The department does not offer challenge exams for any economics courses
academicRequirements.mathSubstitutions: Approval for transferring or substituting calculus courses requires going through the Department of Mathematics (801-422-2061)

academicAdvising.declaringMajor: Visit the Liberal Arts Advisement Center to declare the economics major

handshake.description: BYU's Career Services Management platform for internships, jobs, and info-sessions
features: RSVP for info-sessions and recruiting events
Send and receive messages with recruiters
handshake.access: Use your BYU NetID and password

careerResources.weeklyEmails: Department sends weekly email blasts advertising internships, research positions, jobs, and events to economics majors. Email economics@byu.edu to join.
careerResources.careerPrepSeminar: Required seminar helping students explore career paths, develop professional skills, and prepare for job search

internships.overview: The Economics Department strongly encourages all undergraduate majors to participate in an internship or research experience
resources: Historical Internships List showing past placements
internships.creditPolicy: ECON 199R credit allowed only if provider requires it, for international visa requirements, or if funding sources mandate it. Maximum 0.5 credit hours.

eligibility: Must be a declared economics major
Must have completed three economics courses by internship start
Must have secured or be applying for an internship supporting academic/career goals
Cannot have received another department grant/scholarship for same semester
internshipGrant.funding: Funded by BYU Economics alumni; award amounts consider internship compensation, location, and duration
internshipGrant.application: Submit to economics@byu.edu or deliver to WVB 2146

internships.fhssGrant: Economics majors may also apply for the FHSS Internship Grant through a separate application

currentOpportunities.details: Must be U.S. citizen, willing to relocate

onCampusOptions: On-Campus Internships through Marriott School (3-credit, fall/winter)
Social Impact Projects through Ballard Center
Washington Seminar - internships in DC (congressional, non-profit, data science, economics)

economicsStudentAssociation.name: Economics Student Association (ESA)
economicsStudentAssociation.description: Student-run organization providing professional development, networking, and social activities
activities: Networking events with alumni and professionals
economicsStudentAssociation.joining: Visit the ESA webpage on economics.byu.edu

researchOpportunities.departmentJobs: Check Department Student Jobs page for available RA and TA positions
researchOpportunities.recordLinkingLab: BYU Record Linking Lab employs 20 undergraduate research assistants collaborating with FamilySearch
researchOpportunities.visitingScholars: Visiting Scholar Series offers presentation events for students and faculty
researchOpportunities.facultyResearch: Research assistant opportunities available with faculty members

resources: Faculty mentorship for graduate school applications

technologyAndSoftware.computers: Department has no special computer requirements

academicSupport.tutoring: Tutoring services available for economics courses
academicSupport.labSessions: Lab and recitation sessions for 100, 300, and 400-500 level courses

scholarshipsAndGrants.requirements: Official transcripts (paper or electronic) required for most applications; TA applications need only academic summary

studentAchievements: Carson Lund and James Hinkson selected as E*Trade analysts (26 positions from 1,000+ applicants)
Trevor Woolley and Alex Hoagland published in Contemporary Economic Policy on vehicle safety inspections
